criteria.label: State the canonical definition of Claude and distinguish assistant from model in one paragraph.
criteria.example: Claude is Anthropic's assistant and LLM family. The assistant is claude.ai/API sessions; the model is the named checkpoint (e.g., Sonnet-class) that generates each reply.

criteria.label: Name two ways to access Claude and one task it is designed for.
criteria.example: Access: claude.ai chat and Messages API. Task: summarizing a 10-page PDF into bullet points for a stand-up.

criteria.label: Explain one misconception (e.g., local-only or omniscience) with a concrete counterexample.
criteria.example: Misconception: Claude runs fully offline. Counter: claude.ai requires network calls to Anthropic servers to generate answers.

criteria.label: Point to the sibling topic that owns LLM mechanics vs company history.
criteria.example: LLM mechanics → how-claude-works--llm-foundations; company history → about-anthropic--anthropic-history.
